laws.title: Formation of Telangana State
laws.description: On and from the appointed day, there shall be formed a new State to be known as the State of Telangana comprising the following territories of the existing State of Andhra Pradesh, namely:— Adilabad, Karimnagar, Medak, Nizamabad, Warangal, Rangareddi, Nalgonda, Mahbubnagar, Khammam (as specified) and Hyderabad districts.
laws.simpleExplanation: This section officially creates the state of Telangana by separating specific districts from the original state of Andhra Pradesh.

relatedSections.description: Defines the remaining territories that constitute the State of Andhra Pradesh.
Designates Hyderabad as the common capital for both states for a period not exceeding ten years.

laws.title: Governor of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana
laws.description: On and from the appointed day, the Governor of the existing State of Andhra Pradesh shall be the Governor for both the State of Andhra Pradesh and the State of Telangana for such period as may be determined by the President.
laws.simpleExplanation: After the split, the same person who was the Governor of Andhra Pradesh will serve as the Governor for both new states for a time decided by the President.

laws.title: Provisions as to Legislative Councils
laws.description: On and from the appointed day, there shall be constituted a Legislative Council for each of the successor States of Andhra Pradesh and Telangana.
laws.simpleExplanation: Both Andhra Pradesh and Telangana will have their own Legislative Councils (Upper Houses) to help make laws for their respective states.

relatedSections.description: Specifies the number of seats in the Legislative Councils for both states.

laws.title: High Court of Judicature at Hyderabad to be common High Court
laws.description: On and from the appointed day,— (a) the High Court of Judicature at Hyderabad shall be the common High Court for the State of Telangana and the State of Andhra Pradesh till a separate High Court for the State of Andhra Pradesh is constituted.
laws.simpleExplanation: The existing High Court in Hyderabad will serve both states until a brand new High Court is built specifically for Andhra Pradesh.

relatedSections.description: Provisions for the establishment of a separate High Court for the State of Andhra Pradesh.
